title: rubroshorea coriacea
intro: Rubroshorea coriacea, also known as Red Sandalwood, is a plant species native to Australia and Asia, characterized by its distinctive red bark and heartwood. The medicinal properties of Rubroshorea coriacea are attributed to its rich content of santalols, which have been traditionally used to treat a variety of ailments. The health benefits of this plant include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce pain and inflammation, and combat infections. Rubroshorea coriacea is commonly prepared as an essential oil or a resin, which can be used in aromatherapy, topical applications, or added to herbal remedies.
